title: Linting for Correctness, Safety, and Maintainability · Policy Recall
summary: Configure a high-signal linter beyond default style warnings. Learners retrieve rules and procedures before seeing feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a risk-based lint policy and fixtures after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Start with maintained recommended rules and promote material findings to errors.
Enable rules for invalid control flow, unsafe APIs, promises, hooks, and accessibility as applicable.
Set severity from failure impact and confidence rather than preference strength.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# Risk-profiled repository gate portfolio
# Each gate needs purpose, command, failure fixture, owner, and suppression policy.

steps.title: Retrieve the complete evidence cycle
steps.instruction: Order the Recommended Baseline evidence cycle before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Recommended Baseline assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

content.text: Start with maintained recommended rules and promote material findings to errors. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Check preconditions, perform the operation in an inspectable order, then verify the result and a boundary case.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Use a changed worked example case derived from this module: gates lint correctness. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. A defensible solution applies Recommended Baseline, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a risk-based lint policy and fixtures.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Recommended rules cover every repository risk. This fails the Recommended Baseline contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

content.label: Research anchor
content.value: Ruff Rules (Current 2026-07-13) — Controls Python linting, unused code, safe fixes, suppression review, and rule selection.

content.label: Mastery evidence
content.value: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Start with maintained recommended rules and promote material findings to errors.

Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: Recommended rules cover every repository risk.

options.text: Define the stakeholder decision, quantities, authority, and constraints
Choose and document the smallest defensible model or procedure
Execute the model while preserving inspectable intermediate evidence
Test boundaries and changed cases, communicate limits, and revise

stimulus.title: Lint Upgrades case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: Use a changed review case derived from this module: gates lint correctness. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: Linter upgrades are behavior-free dependency bumps.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Review new, removed, renamed, and changed rules during tool upgrades. Artifact under review: a risk-based lint policy and fixtures.

options.text: The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.
The record names the assumption, applies Lint Upgrades, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

steps.title: Record what required effort
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Lint Upgrades applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Review new, removed, renamed, and changed rules during tool upgrades.

hints: Name the Lint Upgrades precondition or invariant that the review case must preserve.
